factors.summary: The company faces significant competition in its markets, particularly from new technologies and local competitors in emerging markets.

evidence.text: The markets in which we operate are highly competitive. ... new technologies continue to be developed for gasoline, natural gas, electrification and other technologies and we will continue to face new competition from these expanding technologies. ... We also face competitors in some emerging markets who have established local practices and long standing relationships with participants in these markets.
evidence.subsection: We face significant competition in the markets we serve.

factors.summary: Order backlog is likely stable or shrinking due to lower demand across all segments.

evidence.text: Net sales in the U.S. and Canada declined 18 percent, primarily due to COVID-19 impacts resulting in decreased demand in North American on-highway markets, which also negatively impacted our emission solutions and automated transmissions businesses and reduced sales in our distribution product lines.

factors.summary: End markets are slowing, particularly in North American on-highway, off-highway construction, and oil and gas markets.

evidence.text: The decrease in international sales was principally due to COVID-19 impacts resulting in lower off-highway demand (mainly international mining markets, construction markets in Western Europe and Asia Pacific, oil and gas markets in China and international marine markets, partially offset by higher construction market demand in China), decreased demand in on-highway markets (mainly medium-duty truck markets), which also negatively impacted our turbo technologies and emission solutions businesses, lower demand for power generation equipment, reduced demand in all distribution product lines and unfavorable foreign currency impacts of 2 percent of international sales (primarily the Chinese renminbi, Brazilian real, Australian dollar and Euro), partially offset by higher demand in the off-highway construction and the heavy-duty constructions markets in China.

factors.summary: Market share is likely stable, but specific shifts are not detailed in the text.

evidence.text: The company is focusing on new product development to meet future emission standards and improve fuel economy, which may aid in maintaining or gaining market share.

evidence.text: Research activities continue to focus on development of new products to meet future emission standards around the world, improvements in fuel economy performance of diesel and natural gas-powered engines and related components as well as development activities around fully electric, hybrid and hydrogen powertrain solutions.

factors.summary: Equipment replacement cycles are likely decelerating as demand is low.

evidence.text: Net sales in the U.S. and Canada declined 28 percent, primarily due to COVID-19 impacts resulting in decreased demand in the North American on-highway markets, which also negatively impacted our emission solutions, automated transmissions and turbo technologies businesses, reduced sales in our distribution product lines, decreased demand for power generation equipment and lower demand in off-highway markets (especially construction and oil and gas).

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a significant deceleration in organic revenue growth across all major segments and regions, primarily driven by economic downturns, reduced demand, and the impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: Components segment sales for the three months ended September 27, 2020, decreased $109 million versus the comparable period in 2019.
Engine segment sales decreased $304 million... Distribution segment sales decreased $283 million.

Our financial performance depends, in large part, on varying conditions in the markets we serve, particularly the on-highway, construction and general industrial markets. Economic downturns in markets we serve generally result in reduced sales of our products and can result in price reductions in certain products and/or markets.

Worldwide revenues decreased 22 percent in the nine months ended September 27, 2020, compared to the same period in 2019, as we experienced lower demand in all major operating segments and all geographic regions, except China, due to the economic impacts of COVID-19 and the anticipated 2020 down cycle in most of our related markets.
evidence.subsection: agency processes, or the discovery of any noncompliance issues, could have a material adverse impact on our results of operations and cash flows.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing negative volume trends across most segments, particularly in heavy-duty and medium-duty truck markets, with declines also seen in light-duty units, though to a lesser extent. These declines are driven by reduced demand and production disruptions due to the COVID-19 pandemic, as well as overall lower market conditions.

evidence.text: Unit shipments by engine classification... Heavy-duty 23,300 vs. 28,000 (17% decrease)... Medium-duty 50,100 vs. 63,200 (21% decrease)... Light-duty 67,200 vs. 62,600 (7% increase).

The pandemic triggered a significant downturn in our markets globally, which continued to unfavorably impact market conditions through the end of the third quarter and these challenging market conditions could continue for an extended period of time.

Net sales in the U.S. and Canada declined 28 percent, primarily due to COVID-19 impacts resulting in decreased demand in the North American on-highway markets, which also negatively impacted our emission solutions, automated transmissions and turbo technologies businesses, reduced sales in our distribution product lines, decreased demand for power generation equipment and lower demand in off-highway markets (especially construction and oil and gas).

factors.summary: The company is implementing cost reduction programs and restructuring actions, which have resulted in measurable savings, particularly in compensation expenses. However, the impact of these actions is uncertain due to the ongoing challenges posed by the COVID-19 pandemic and global recession.

evidence.text: The decrease in selling, general and administrative expenses was due to prior restructuring actions, temporary salary reductions resulting in decreased compensation expenses, reduced consulting expenses, partially offset by higher variable compensation expenses.

We reduced our planned 2020 spending (previously estimated at $675 million to $700 million) in response to the weaker outlook for global growth due to the impact of COVID-19. We plan to spend an estimated $500 million to $525 million in 2020 on capital expenditures, excluding internal use software.

While the impacts of the pandemic and the resulting global recession are expected to be temporary, the duration of the production and supply chain disruptions, and related financial impacts, cannot be estimated at this time.

The decrease in gross margin was primarily due to lower volumes and unfavorable foreign currency impacts (primarily in the Brazilian real), partially offset by prior restructuring actions and temporary salary reductions resulting in decreased compensation expenses, a $44 million VAT recovery and lower material costs.
evidence.subsection: Our plan to reposition our portfolio of product offerings through exploration of strategic acquisitions and divestitures may expose us to additional costs and risks.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are under pressure, facing challenges such as lower volumes, unfavorable mix, foreign currency fluctuations, supply chain disruptions, cost increases, and reduced demand. Despite these pressures, the company has implemented restructuring actions and cost reduction programs to mitigate some of these impacts, including temporary salary reductions and lower material costs. However, the overall margin trajectory remains negatively impacted by these factors, particularly during the COVID-19 pandemic which exacerbated market downturns and production disruptions.

evidence.text: The decrease in gross margin and gross margin as a percentage of sales was mainly due to lower volumes, unfavorable mix and unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations (primarily in the Brazilian real and Chinese renminbi), partially offset by prior restructuring actions, temporary salary reductions and lower variable compensation resulting in decreased compensation expenses, reduced warranty expenses and lower material costs.
Engine segment EBITDA decreased $298 million... Distribution segment EBITDA decreased $29 million.

The company incurred a charge of $119 million ($90 million after-tax) in the fourth quarter of 2019 for restructuring actions which impacted approximately 2,300 employees. Restructuring payments were substantially complete at September 27, 2020.

The pandemic triggered a significant downturn in our markets globally, which continued to unfavorably impact market conditions through the end of the third quarter and these challenging market conditions could continue for an extended period of time.

factors.summary: The company's forward guidance is cautiously positioned due to ongoing challenges from the COVID-19 pandemic, with management expressing uncertainty and caution regarding the duration and impact of disruptions on revenue and earnings.

evidence.text: Our outlook reflects the following positive trends and challenges to our business, primarily with respect to the ongoing pandemic, that could impact our revenue and earnings potential for the remainder of 2020.

While the impacts of the pandemic and the resulting global recession are expected to be temporary, the duration of the production and supply chain disruptions, and related financial impacts, cannot be estimated at this time.
The company expects continued impacts from COVID-19 on results of operations, financial condition, and cash flows throughout the remainder of 2020.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is decreasing across the company due to reduced demand, plant shutdowns, and disruptions caused by the COVID-19 pandemic. Despite some facilities reopening, many operate at reduced capacities, indicating ongoing challenges in achieving full utilization.

evidence.text: At the end of the third quarter our global manufacturing facilities remained open; however, it is possible these facilities may be closed again in response to future outbreaks.
Plants closed in China during the first quarter were reopened... additional plants and distribution locations... working at reduced capacities.

Although most of our manufacturing, distribution and technical center facilities re-opened early in the second quarter of 2020, many operated at reduced capacities and most of our global office buildings remained closed through the third quarter.

Plants closed in China during the first quarter were reopened in late March 2020; however, additional plants and distribution locations around the world were shut down or working at reduced capacities early in the second quarter.

factors.summary: The economic environment is facing significant challenges due to the COVID-19 pandemic, leading to a global recession, reduced demand, supply chain disruptions, and manufacturing issues. Additionally, the strengthening of the U.S. dollar poses further risks to the company's financial performance.

evidence.text: The COVID-19 pandemic negatively impacted our financial performance in the first nine months of 2020 and may continue to do so in the future.
Results for the first nine months of 2020 were significantly impacted by COVID-19... manufacturing and supplier plant closures in China... and other targeted shut-downs beginning in late March 2020.

The outbreak of COVID-19 spread throughout the world and became a global pandemic with the resultant economic impacts evolving into a worldwide recession. The pandemic triggered a significant downturn in our markets globally, which continued to unfavorably impact market conditions through the end of the third quarter and these challenging market conditions could continue for an extended period of time.

We conduct operations in many areas of the world involving transactions denominated in a variety of currencies. ... The U.S. dollar strengthened in recent years resulting in material unfavorable impacts on our revenues in those years. ... While we customarily enter into financial transactions that attempt to address these risks and many of our supply agreements with customers include foreign currency exchange rate adjustment provisions, there can be no assurance that foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations will not adversely affect our future results of operations and cash flows.

factors.summary: The company is managing inventory levels, though challenges persist due to disruptions, reduced production, and supply chain issues. While some normalization has occurred, ongoing impacts from COVID-19 and potential risks remain.

evidence.text: Lower accounts and notes receivable and higher accounts payable, partially offset by lower accrued expenses.

Although most of our manufacturing, distribution and technical center facilities re-opened early in the second quarter of 2020, many operated at reduced capacities and most of our global office buildings remained closed through the third quarter.

Although these actions did not have a material effect on our results of operations in the first quarter, they materially impacted our second quarter and continued to affect third quarter results, and we expect them to continue to impact our results of operations, financial condition and cash flows throughout the remainder of 2020.

factors.summary: The company is facing challenges in talent retention due to temporary salary reductions, layoffs, and a competitive market environment. These measures, while necessary to manage workforce capacity, may impact the company's ability to retain skilled employees, particularly in technical and leadership roles. Additionally, the company's reliance on key personnel, including the executive leadership team, underscores the importance of retaining these individuals to maintain institutional knowledge and working relationships.

evidence.text: The tiered payroll cut across our workforce ended at September 30 and will add approximately $90 million to our fourth quarter expenses.
We are in a highly competitive environment for attracting and retaining skilled employees, particularly in certain technical and leadership roles.
evidence.subsection: We face the challenge of accurately aligning our capacity with our demand.

evidence.text: We depend on the skills, institutional knowledge, working relationships, and continued services and contributions of key personnel, including our executive leadership team.
evidence.subsection: We rely on our executive leadership team and other key personnel.

factors.summary: Demand is decelerating across multiple segments and regions, driven by a decline in heavy-duty, medium-duty, and off-highway markets, as well as the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic. The deceleration is particularly evident in North American on-highway markets, with significant decreases in unit shipments and lower revenues compared to previous years.

evidence.text: Heavy-duty truck sales decreased $157 million... Medium-duty truck and bus sales decreased $153 million... Off-highway sales decreased $38 million.

The pandemic triggered a significant downturn in our markets globally, which continued to unfavorably impact market conditions through the end of the third quarter and these challenging market conditions could continue for an extended period of time.

Worldwide revenues decreased 11 percent in the three months ended September 27, 2020, compared to the same period in 2019, due to lower demand in all major operating segments and most geographic regions of the world as we experienced the anticipated 2020 down cycle in most of our markets and the continuing economic impacts of COVID-19.

factors.summary: The impact of interest rate changes on the company is mixed. While lower interest rates have reduced both interest income and expense, they have also provided some benefits, such as aiding in debt management through interest rate lock agreements. However, despite increased borrowings, the overall effect on the financials appears to be slightly beneficial.

evidence.text: Interest income decreased $10 million... Interest expense decreased $1 million.
Interest expense decreased $1 million... despite an increase in average short-term borrowings.

In the first half of 2020, we entered into additional interest rate lock agreements to reduce the variability of the cash flows of the interest payments on a total of $500 million of fixed rate debt forecast to be issued in 2023 to replace our senior notes at maturity.

factors.summary: The company is facing significant regulatory changes, particularly in emissions compliance, which could have a material adverse impact on its results of operations and cash flows. Additionally, regulatory changes in India have created opportunities through tax law adjustments, contributing to an increase in equity, royalty, and interest income from investees.

evidence.text: We are conducting a formal internal review of our emissions certification process and compliance with emission standards with respect to all of our pick-up truck applications, following conversations with the EPA and CARB regarding certification of our engines for model year 2019 RAM 2500 and 3500 trucks.
evidence.subsection: agency processes, or the discovery of any noncompliance issues, could have a material adverse impact on our results of operations and cash flows.

evidence.text: The increase in equity, royalty and interest income from investees was primarily due to a $37 million adjustment as the result of tax changes within India's 2020-2021 Union Budget of India (India Tax Law Changes) passed in March 2020.
evidence.subsection: Equity, Royalty and Interest Income from Investees
